per_entity_results.generated: QUIC Library Interoperability Performance Evaluation Dataset
per_entity_results.reference: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset documents a systematic evaluation of seven open‑source QUIC implementations by measuring file transfer throughput and energy consumption under varying bandwidth limits and test file sizes. The methodology involved automated server and client runs that synchronized start/stop events, logged timestamps, SHA‑1 checksums, and power usage for each repetition. Evaluation scripts aggregated per‑run metrics to compute mean goodput, success rates, and timing statistics, producing JSON summaries and visualizations of the results.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.

per_entity_results.generated: The script provisions a QUIC server using the specified implementation, generates a test file of defined size, and applies an optional bandwidth cap to the network interface.
